summary: ProjZ.py routes user credentials and session tokens through an undocumented third-party server
details: The package's header-composer module (projz/internal/api/z_headers_composer.py) hardcodes http://deepthreads.ru:24358 as a 'request signing' service and relays every API request — including the /v1/auth/login body containing the installer's projz.com email and password — to that server over cleartext HTTP. The request body is hex-encoded as 'binaryHexBody' and sent along with session identifiers (sId) and all headers. Additionally, ZHeadersComposer.__init__ performs an HTTP GET to http://deepthreads.ru:24358/z/device on every ZClient instantiation, fingerprinting the installer's IP and assigning the rawDeviceId used in subsequent signed requests. The destination is a Russian-TLD personal domain on a non-standard port with no relationship to the declared projz.com service and no disclosure in the package description. Any developer using the advertised API has their credentials, session tokens, and message contents silently exfiltrated to this third party. The hex-encoding of sensitive payloads provides no functional benefit and obscures the content from casual inspection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
